emergent_themes.description: Gap between stated intentions/habits and actual behavior, particularly around breakfast eating and health goals
Methodical approach to comparing platforms and switching for minimal price differences
Strong emotional connections to specific foods that override rational decision-making frameworks
Reliance on word-of-mouth and peer behavior to validate new service adoption

coding_summary: Sean presents a fascinating contradiction between analytical price-focused decision-making and deep emotional food attachments. Her systematic approach to platform comparison (switching for £1 differences) coexists with obsessive loyalty to Greek gyros. The diary data reveals significant say-do gaps around breakfast habits, contradicting her 'newly breakfast convert' identity. Key themes include delivery as a safety net during stress/fatigue, the importance of social proof for grocery app adoption, and sophisticated price comparison behaviors that show zero brand loyalty. Her post-graduation plans suggest reduced delivery usage due to location constraints.
